question_text: A light wave has a speed of 3 × 10⁸ m/s and a wavelength of 6 × 10⁻⁷ m. Calculate its frequency and determine what colour this light is.
correct_answer: Frequency = 5 × 10¹⁴ Hz. This is red light (wavelengths of 6-7 × 10⁻⁷ m produce red light).
explanation: Using frequency = speed ÷ wavelength: frequency = (3 × 10⁸) ÷ (6 × 10⁻⁷) = 5 × 10¹⁴ Hz. In the visible spectrum, this frequency corresponds to red light, which has the longest wavelength of visible light.
